author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Design doc shipped at `docs/agent-await-gates-design.md`
(commit `d23a50c`, on the worktree branch — landing on
`main` next). The doc answers all five research questions:

1. Worth the complexity? No — the auto-clear is the only
   thing full gates buy over a simpler `Status::Blocked`,
   and the cost (polling architecture, auth dependency,
   hostname-namespace decision, network in the CLI) is too
   high for use cases that have come up zero times so far.
2. Polling architecture: pull-out-of-band (`jjf check-gates`)
   would be the right choice IF we shipped this. But we're
   not.
3. v1 gate types: would have been `timer` and `fj:pr`. Not
   shipping either.
4. Spec impact: a new `Status` variant is cleaner than a
   flag on `Open`; v2.5 bump mirrors the v2.3 `InProgress`
   pattern.
5. Sequencing: `agent-claim-atomic` (`c3cc807`) already
   shipped at v2.3; the followup ticket is a clean v2.5 bump
   on its own. No folding.

beads' shape is documented (`reference/beads/internal/types/`
`types.go` lines 92–96, status enum lines 327–334;
`reference/beads/cmd/bd/gate.go` for the polling verb;
`reference/beads/cmd/bd/defer.go` for the `--until` /
`--reason` flag shape).

# Recommendation

**Skip the full gates feature.** Ship `Status::Blocked` plus
`jjf block <id> [--reason <text>]` in a v2.5 spec bump.

Follow-up implementation ticket filed: **`8ddf3fb`**
(`agent-await-gates-impl`).

# Confidence

Moderately high. The slim approach is a strict subset of
the full approach, so extending it later (to add
`await_type` / `await_id` fields for auto-clearing gates)
doesn't require a rewrite. The risk is that we later wish
we had auto-clearing gates and have to pay the polling cost
then; the design doc lists the three signals that would
re-open the question.

# Open follow-ups

- `8ddf3fb` (agent-await-gates-impl) — the actual v2.5 spec
  bump + `Status::Blocked` + `jjf block` verb.
- None other. The research is settled.
